anonymousSessionNotes: Isolated context form-path-anon-2. localStorage auth.access_token = null/empty. Cookies: GA + AppInsights analytics only (no auth cookie). Page stayed on /account/impersonate/:userId — NOT redirected to /sign-in.

BL_UI_001.note: Post-paint CLS only (PerformanceObserver with buffered:true, installed after navigation). 1 shift observed — likely logo/font swap. Under 0.1 threshold.

BL_UI_002.note: All form structural spacing on-grid. Container padding: 24px (on-grid). Email .vc-input wrapper marginBottom: 16px (on-grid). Password .vc-input wrapper marginBottom: 16px (on-grid). Button paddingLeft/Right: 16px (on-grid). Only 1px browser-default margins on input[type] elements — intentional outline/border, not a design token.

BL_UI_005.labelAboveInputNote: Label center-Y vs input center-Y drift = 34px — by design (label is block-stacked above field, not inline). Not a BL-UI-005 violation.

BL_UI_006.note: BL-UI-006 applies at all viewports for interactive elements explicitly listed in invariant spec

BL_UI_003.note: Filling email + password fields caused zero positional shift. All rects identical to empty baseline.

states.errorText: Login attempt failed. Check your credentials

BL_UI_001.note: CLS from error block insertion = 0.003 — well under threshold. The PerformanceObserver records the shift value but it is tiny.

BL_UI_002.note: VcAlert component uses non-grid padding values (5.008/7.008/10px). These appear to be fractional Tailwind values (e.g. py-[5px], px-[10px] or similar) rather than grid-compliant tokens. Affects all vc-alert--outline-dark instances, not specific to this form.

BL_UI_003.note: Error block inserted above the form fields (between description text and Email label). Form grows in height by 46px. All form content shifts DOWN by 23px. Form container itself shifts UP by 23px to accommodate the alert. This is a push-layout (not overlay), causing measurable neighbour shift.
BL_UI_003.fix: Reserve space for the error alert before submit (e.g. min-height on form, or always-rendered hidden placeholder). Or use absolute/overlay positioning for the error that does not push layout.

iconTextAlignment.note: Icon and error text in vc-alert share identical center-Y. Perfect alignment.

BL_UI_006.note: Same 3 elements as empty state. No additional affordances in error alert (no Try Again link). Error alert itself has no interactive elements.
BL_UI_006.passwordClearNote: Password field NOT cleared after failed auth — per IMP-003 spec check. This may be intentional (UX: allow retry without retyping) or a spec violation depending on security policy.

states.note: Chrome DevTools MCP minimum viewport width appears to be 500px — resize to 375 resolved to 500. 500px is within the ≤768 mobile range so BL-UI-006 still applies.

tooClose.note: Password input right edge and show/hide button left edge are 1px apart — below the 8px gap threshold

pageDescription: You are logging in on behalf of a customer. The session will be audited.
formContract.errorMessage: Login attempt failed. Check your credentials

findings.description: Inline error insertion (empty→error state) pushes all form content down by 23px and expands the form container by 46px. This is a state-induced layout shift — content below the form (footer, page elements) shifts on every failed login attempt.
findings.fix: Pre-reserve vertical space for the error block (min-height or always-rendered hidden placeholder), or use overlay/absolute positioning.

findings.description: VcAlert component (vc-alert--outline-dark--dang) has off-grid padding: paddingTop/Bottom = 5.008px (expected 4 or 8), paddingRight = 10px (expected 8 or 12), paddingLeft = 7.008px (expected 8). Affects all danger-variant alerts across the storefront, not isolated to this form.

Three undersized touch targets in ImpersonateForm: Email input (h=36px, deficit -8px), Password input (h=36px, deficit -8px), Show/hide password toggle (38x38px, deficit -6px on both axes). At mobile (500px), password input and show/hide toggle are 1px apart (< 8px gap threshold). The two action buttons (Verify/Cancel) meet the 44px height requirement.
findings.note: Input height defect is a VcInput size='md' design token issue shared across all md-size inputs in the storefront.
